title: ARRAYFORMULA vs dynamic arrays: the same idea, three dialects
meta_desc: Google Sheets ARRAYFORMULA vs Excel/LibreOffice auto-spilling dynamic arrays: portability, syntax differences, and what breaks when you export.
intro: Google Sheets makes formulas operate over whole ranges by wrapping them in ARRAYFORMULA; modern Excel and LibreOffice instead auto-spill any array expression. Same concept, incompatible spellings — and the number-one thing that silently breaks when a Sheet is exported to .xlsx.

when.use_when: Sheets-only workbooks. If the file might ever export to Excel/LibreOffice, prefer the dynamic-array functions (FILTER, UNIQUE, SEQUENCE...) that exist on both sides, and avoid wrapping plain arithmetic.
Excel 365/2021 and LibreOffice 24.8+ — write the array expression directly. For files that must open in genuinely old versions, neither dialect survives; stick to fill-down formulas.

compat_note: From our executed tests: the shared dynamic-array functions (FILTER, SORT, UNIQUE, SEQUENCE, XLOOKUP...) work in LibreOffice 24.8+ and are documented in both Excel 365 and Sheets — they are the portable core. ARRAYFORMULA itself exists only in Google Sheets; on export, Sheets rewrites what it can, but wrapped arithmetic frequently degrades to a single-cell result.
